proven.claim: PROVEN: At line 165, fileIssue() returned without throwing
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 165
; PROVEN: At line 165, fileIssue() returned without throwing
(declare-const file_issue_threw Int)
(declare-const reached_line_165 Int)
; Code transition: line 165 is inside try{} before catch — execution reaches it
; only if fileIssue did not throw
(assert (=> (= file_issue_threw 1) (= reached_line_165 0)))
; Code transition: we are executing line 165
(assert (= reached_line_165 1))
; Negation: fileIssue threw but line 165 was reached anyway
(assert (= file_issue_threw 1))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: fileIssue()'s precondition (issue does not exist) is not reliably established.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P1
; LINE: 165
; VIOLATION: fileIssue()'s precondition (issue does not exist) is not reliably established.
; issueExists() catch block returns false on ANY exception — a network/auth failure
; makes the predicate false-negative, causing fileIssue to create a duplicate.
(declare-const issue_truly_exists Int)
(declare-const gh_cli_threw_exception Int)
(declare-const issue_exists_returned Int)
(declare-const file_issue_called Int)
; Code transition: issueExists catch block swallows all errors and returns false
(assert (= gh_cli_threw_exception 1))
(assert (=> (= gh_cli_threw_exception 1) (= issue_exists_returned 0)))
; Code transition: the issue genuinely exists on GitHub despite the exception
(assert (= issue_truly_exists 1))
; Code transition: fileViolationIssues calls fileIssue when issueExists returns false
(assert (=> (= issue_exists_returned 0) (= file_issue_called 1)))
; Violation: fileIssue invoked when issue already exists → GitHub duplicate created
(assert (= file_issue_called 1))
(assert (= issue_truly_exists 1))
(check-sat)
